understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to get pushable objects (blocks) onto goal tiles marked with '+' on the semantic grid. Each level required satisfying a win condition by positioning blocks correctly on the designated goal positions.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement used pixel coordinates [x, y] on a 3-pixels-per-cell grid. The player could MOVE in four directions, which would shift the selected piece and potentially push adjacent pushable objects. Walls blocked movement in various ways. Multiple selectable pieces could be controlled by using CLICK with their pixel coordinates to switch focus. The level had a step budget that decreased with each action. Movement hints indicated blocked directions, but attempting actions was necessary to learn actual behavior since hints were heuristic-only.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to get pushable objects (blocks) onto goal tiles marked with '+' on the semantic grid. Each level required satisfying a win condition by positioning blocks correctly on the designated goal positions.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement used pixel coordinates [x, y] on a 3-pixels-per-cell grid. The player could MOVE in four directions, which would shift the selected piece and potentially push adjacent pushable objects. Walls blocked movement in various ways. Multiple selectable pieces could be controlled by using CLICK with their pixel coordinates to switch focus. The level had a step budget that decreased with each action. Movement hints indicated blocked directions, but attempting actions was necessary to learn actual behavior since hints were heuristic-only.
